texto: «Zaki never grew tired of looking at the whiteness of Roar’s hair. It was as if the moon and the stars had taken root in it. And perhaps they had, so long ago when Roar travelled amongst them in his space shuttle.»From "The Stars Seem So Far Away
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Margrét Helgadóttir: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “«Zaki never grew tired of looking at the whiteness of Roar’s hair. It was as if the moon and the …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, space, stars pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e reflexao.
